beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check weather and rooftop garden for heat stress.
beats.reasoning: Mateo wakes early as usual; yesterday's moderate energy (55/100) and meditative evening mean he's rested. Hot forecast prompts an early start to beat the heat and water garden if needed.

beats.activity: Bike commute to the shop, stopping to check tomato plants for wilting.
beats.reasoning: Mateo rides to work; the 40-min commute allows a mental warm-up and gives him time to assess heat-stress risk to his urban garden as he passes neighborhoods.

beats.activity: Bike mechanic work: tune-ups, derailleur adjustments, customer consultations.
beats.reasoning: Core work block; Georgetown shop is quiet enough on Wednesdays that Mateo can focus on technical work and build client relationships without rush.

beats.activity: Lunch at a cool, shaded spot—Vietnamese pho or banh mi to stay hydrated.
beats.reasoning: Heat day (91°F) + air quality concerns make a cool indoor meal with broth appealing; Mateo frequents this spot and values hydration on hot days.
Short walk to return to work; keeps him within the Georgetown area.

beats.activity: Afternoon shop work: more consultations, bike builds, prep for weekend cycling guides.
beats.reasoning: Workaholic tendency means Mateo pushes through the afternoon heat; uses time to prep weekend group ride logistics and talk clients through upgrades.

beats.activity: Bike commute home; stop at a local thrift store in Wallingford for transit-map browsing.
beats.reasoning: Mateo collects transit maps and thrifts regularly; Wallingford is en route from Georgetown to Northgate, and a brief stop breaks up the ride while the heat is still intense.

beats.activity: Bike ride to Gas Works Park, arriving early before the 6pm concert crowd.
beats.reasoning: Hot day + free outdoor concert is a draw, but Mateo values close friends over big crowds; arriving early lets him secure a calm spot and hydrate before the 6pm concert fills up.

beats.activity: Enjoy the outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; socialize with a close friend or two if they show; cool down with water.
beats.reasoning: Mateo is generous and social with close friends; the concert is free and aligns with his evening vibe; the park's height and breeze offer some relief from the heat.

beats.activity: Bike home to Northgate as the light softens and heat breaks.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute home; the cooling evening and fading light make for a pleasant ride, aligning with Mateo's love of the golden-hour transition.

beats.activity: Tend rooftop garden; water tomatoes and check heat damage; deadhead spent flowers; catalog new thrift finds.
beats.reasoning: After a hot day, the rooftop garden needs attention; Mateo finds this meditative and uses the cooler evening to water and assess plant health. Cataloging transit maps is his wind-down ritual.

beats.activity: Relax at home: light dinner, beer, reading about transit history or cycling routes.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep; Mateo is already home and settles into a quiet evening of food, drink, and reading—his preferred way to let the day settle.
Full night's rest (7 hours 15 min) after a moderately active day; ending before 06:00 as required.

diary:
Woke up anxious about the heat—91 degrees today, brutal. Checked the rooftop garden first thing; the tomatoes looked stressed but holding on. The ride to the shop in Georgetown was fine while it was still cool, though I could smell that wildfire smoke from BC hanging in the air.

Work was steady. Lots of tune-ups, a few derailleur fixes, and I spent time prepping weekend route notes for the cycling guides. Grabbed pho at Thanh Huong around midday—needed the broth and electrolytes. The afternoon dragged a bit in the heat, but I pushed through.

Stopped at the Wallingford thrift on the way home, found an old transit map from the 80s. Felt like a small win. Got to Gas Works early before the concert crowd hit, caught some live music with the city stretched out below, everything golden in that pre-sunset light. It was perfect, actually. Rode home as things cooled down.

Spent the evening watering the garden, deadheading flowers, cataloging my thrift finds. Now just beer and a book about old Seattle bike routes. Tired but satisfied.
thought_process: I was worried about the heat affecting the garden from the moment I woke—that's always my first concern on days like this. The smoke didn't bother me too much; I'm used to it. Work felt routine but necessary; I needed those afternoon hours to prep properly for weekend guiding. I chose to eat light and hydrating at lunch because I knew the day would only get hotter. The thrift stop was pure impulse—I needed something to break up the commute, something tactile and grounding. Going to Gas Works early was strategic; I wanted the calm before the crowds, and I got it. The music and light lifted my mood significantly after a long day in the shop. By evening, tending the garden felt restorative, like closing a loop. I was tired but grounded.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
